text.title: event-sourcing-versioning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for event-sourcing-versioning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Copying command payload directly into event fields without a PII audit step — e.g. OrderPlaced(shipping_address={"name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com", ...}).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shipping addresses and emails are personal data. Once in the immutable event log without crypto-shredding, they cannot be erased. A future GDPR erasure request becomes technically impossible to satisfy without corrupting the event store.

failure-mode.description: Adding a field to a published event class "just to make a query easier" without bumping event_version and registering an upcaster.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consumers reading historical events receive old-shape events. If they expect the new field, they get AttributeError or None silently. The version bump + upcaster is the contract that allows consumers to upgrade independently.

failure-mode.description: Deleting or renaming a published event class without a coordinated multi-consumer deprecation and replacement plan.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consumers depend on the event class name for routing and deserialization. Silent removal breaks all consumers simultaneously. Human review is required — never auto-merge event class deletions.
